title: Vibrant Roasted Moroccan Lamb Bissara
description: A hearty and flavorful Moroccan dish featuring roasted lamb and a unique cheese-pull effect, served in an agave leaf vessel and paired with crunchy cookies for a satisfying meal prep option. This flexitarian-friendly recipe is perfect for a special occasion or a cozy dinner.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the lamb shoulder with paprika, cumin, salt, and pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat and sear the lamb until browned on all sides.
Add the chopped onion, minced garlic, and grated ginger to the pot and cook until the onion is translucent.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and roast the lamb for 2 1/2 hours, or until tender.
While the lamb is cooking, prepare the agave leaves by cleaning and drying them thoroughly.
Once the lamb is done, remove it from the oven and let it rest for 10 minutes before shredding it into bite-sized pieces.
To assemble the bissara, place a portion of the shredded lamb onto an agave leaf, followed by a spoonful of crumbly feta cheese and a few moroccan cookies.
Serve the bissara hot, garnished with fresh herbs and a side of crunchy cookies.
Repeat the assembly process with the remaining ingredients and store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
